[90-M3] Motion: Add to the text of Unicode 3.2, the following guidelines for the handling of variation selectors: [L2/01-478, L2/02-002] 1) If a variation selector sequence is not available, the variation selector should be ignored. 2) The standardization or support of a particular variation selector does not limit the set of glyphs that can be used to represent the character alone. 3) If you want to have distinction between a character and a character plus a variation selector, you must have a font available that supports that distinction.
[90-A34] Action Item for Mark Davis, Editorial Committee: Add to Unicode 3.2 text on the handling of variation selectors described in motion 90-M3 . [L2/01-478, L2/02-002]

[94-M1] Motion: When a sequence of default grapheme clusters are linked by a combining grapheme joiner, an enclosing mark may be rendered as enclosing the entire sequence. The target of the enclosing mark is the preceding grapheme cluster or sequence of default grapheme clusters linked by grapheme joiner. The intent of the usage of enclosing marks is on free-standing default grapheme clusters or grapheme clusters linked by grapheme joiner. Clarify this in section 7.7 of the Unicode Standard 4.0. The rendering of enclosing marks in complex cases should have many caveats.[L2/03-026, 027, 028]
[94-A7] Action Item for Ken Whistler, Editorial Committee: Make relevant changes on the scope of enclosing marks (from 94-M1) in sections 3.11, 7.7, and 15.2. [L2/03-026, 027, 028]

[96-M4] Motion: With reference to the proposal by ISO CPSG to charge fees for the commercial use of ISO codes: ISO infrastructure standards such as ISO 3166 (country codes), ISO 4217 (currency codes), and ISO 639 (language codes) must be royalty-free. The negative consequences of charging royalties would be severe, including strong incentives for companies and other organizations to:
[96-A41] Action Item for Joan Aliprand: Communicate the text of motion 96-M4 on ISO charging fees for commercial use of standards to NISO.
[96-A42] Action Item for Cathy Wissink: Communicate the text of motion 96-M4 on ISO charging fees for commercial use of standards to INCITS and TC 37.
[96-A43] Action Item for Rick McGowan: Communicate the text of motion 96-M4 on ISO charging fees for commercial use of standards to our liaison organizations, including IETF.
[96-M5] Motion: The recent decision by the maintenance agency for ISO 3166 to re-assign "cs" (formerly Czechoslovakia) to Serbia and Montenegro can cause severe problems. Country codes are a fundamental component of modern computing infrastructure: major operating systems, postal services, business applications, identification and security systems, to name a few. Their stability must be guaranteed. Data that is identified by these codes has a shelf life of decades, not five years.
[96-A44] Action Item for Cathy Wissink: Communicate the text of motion 96-M5 on reassigning the cs country code to the maintenance authority and INCITS.
[96-A45] Action Item for Rick McGowan: Communicate the text of motion 96-M5 on reassigning the cs country code to our liaison organizations, including IETF and Unicode members. In the message say that we urge members to contact their national bodies in other countries, especially in the most-affected countries.
[96-A46] Action Item for Cathy Wissink: Communicate the text of motion 96-M5 on reassigning the cs country code to INCITS and TC 37

[100-M2] Motion: In Unicode 4.1, U+00A0 NO-BREAK SPACE is the preferred base as opposed to U+0020 SPACE. For line breaking, U+00A0 NO-BREAK SPACE gets type AL, drop rule LB7a (rule which says a space with a combining mark acts like a symbol). Add U+00A0 NO-BREAK SPACE to "aletter" in Unicode Standard Annex #29: Text Boundaries. U+0020 SPACE is still a base character, but it does not change its properties. [L2/04-234]

[103-C6] Consensus: As soon as the six lowercase characters (103-M2) are included, the UTC will adopt D1 of document L2/05-076 as a stability policy. The effect of this stability policy is that whenever we add uppercase characters in cased scripts, we need to add the corresponding lowercase characters.

[106-C27] Consensus: (1) Change rule L4 of the bidi algorithm in Proposed Update Unicode Standard Annex #9: The Bidirectional Algorithm to exclude the mirroring of characters whose bidi mirrored property is "false", in the absence of a higher level protocol. (2) Add a new HL6 clause which allows a higher level protocol to mirror characters with the bidi mirroring property "false", for historic scripts and associated punctuation, private use characters, and characters in mathematical expressions. (3) From the new HL6 clause, point to the new commentary in section 6.

[110-C45] Consensus: There are serious technical problems with the document ISO/IEC CD 19757-7: Document Schema Definition Languages (DSDL) - Part 7: Character Repertoire Description Language. The grapheme cluster description is unnecessarily restrictive and makes it impossible to describe repertoires containing arbitrary combining marks. The UTC also has serious concerns about IANA registrations, and the registration process is unspecified. [L2/06-397, L2/07-052]

111
[111-C1] Consensus: Commendation -- Dr. Asmus Freytag has recently retired from active participation in Unicode. Asmus has played a key role in the development of Unicode from the very beginning. He made important contributions to the general structure, architecture, and properties of the Unicode Standard. Asmus also drove the effort to define the Unicode character property model, was the author of two Unicode Standard Annexes, and co-author of one other. For years, Asmus has also organized and produced the charts for the standard, together with the program used to generate them. We regret that Asmus will no longer be involved formally in Unicode -- but welcome future feedback on Unicode issues from him insofar as he is able. We wish him well in his future enterprises.

[115-C11] Consensus: In response to the new Korean standared KS X 1026-1:2007, the UTC reaffirms that normalization cannot be changed. The UTC understands that normalization cannot be implemented conformantly to the Unicode standard or to 10646 if done in the way that is specified in KS X 1026-1:2007. To address the concerns raised in the Korean standard, the UTC resolves to initiate a Unicode Technical Report that specifies a preferred representation for Korean text and mechanisms for conversions to that representation. [L2/08-225]

[115-C22] Consensus: Whereas the continued synchronization of Unicode and 10646 is vital to the success of both, and the publication of a version of the Unicode Standard containing Extension C requires fonts sufficient to print in multi-column format for all sources, the UTC urgently requests that the IRG provide fonts for the publication of Extension C in the Unicode Standard. [133-C41] Consensus: The proposal for "Himalayish" does not reflect the principles that the UTC has followed in the encoding of Indic scripts. The document proposes to unify a wide variety of scripts with differing orthographic behaviors, and would, in our opinion, be extremely difficult to implement and would be inconsistent with existing practice. [L2/12-365]
[133-A60] Action Item for Deborah Anderson: Relay the consensus 133-C41 on "Himalayish" to Pat Hall. [L2/12-365]
[133-C42] Consensus: The UTC notes proposals to encode Newar/Nepaalalipi authored by Anshuman Pandey (L2/12-003) and Dev Dass Manandhar (L2/12-349). We encourage all interested parties to work together to further develop a joint proposal to encode this script. After review of these proposals, the UTC thinks that document L2/12-003 better reflects the approach to encoding models that the UTC favors for the encoding of Indic scripts.
[133-A61] Action Item for Deborah Anderson: Relay consensus [133-C42] on encoding Newar/Nepaalalipi to Anshuman Pandey and Dev Dass Manandhar.

[175-C21] Consensus: The UTC will not add further characters to the Unicode Standard with both (a) canonical decompositions, and (b) Bidi_Paired_Bracket_Type ≠ None. Unicode will also not add further characters where the canonical decompositions *contain* characters whose Bidi_Paired_Bracket_Type ≠ None. See L2/23-079 item 4.1.
[175-C22] Consensus: The UTC establishes the above Consensus 175-C21 as a precedent according to TC procedures 10.5.2.

See L2/23-079 item 4.1.

[180-N6] Note: The UTC notes that, under existing stability policies and commitments, it cannot entertain proposals to add PUA code points to the standard, nor to set aside sections of the PUA reserved for specific functions such as more variation selectors. Proposals to encode additional variants using variation selectors should be constrained to use the existing architecture of variation sequences. Note that while VS17-VS256 are required for the definition of ideographic variation sequences (IVS), they are not prevented from sanctioned use in defining other variation sequences using non-ideographic base characters. [Ref. document L2/24-166 section 33 “VSes: 128 User-Defined Variation Selectors”.]
[180-C43] Consensus: The UTC authorizes the release of Unicode 16.0 and synchronized standards UTS #10, UTS #39, UTS #46 and UTS #51, incorporating the results of decisions and actions as recorded during UTC #180, with a target release date of September 10, 2024.

183
[183-C1] Consensus: The UTC delegates to its technical Working Groups the authorization to create and to post for public review proposed updates to Unicode technical reports that are maintained by the respective UTC working group, and to update such proposed updates with proposed changes, with the following conditions: Any substantial technical changes that have yet to be approved by the UTC shall be noted in a review note in the proposed update; and for technical changes that have already been approved by UTC, that should also be noted in review notes or in the “Modifications” section. For these purposes, substantive technical changes include changes requiring technical decisions as defined in the Unicode Technical Group Procedures. A specific decision is required to approve such changes prior to the approval of the technical report for final publication.
[183-N1] Note: The working groups currently responsible for technical reports are:
[183-N2] Note: The UTC is responsible for decisions about any changes to the working groups responsible for technical reports. In particular, the UTC will normally assign a working group as maintainers for any newly authorized technical report.
[183-N3] Note: Public Review Issues created by the working groups should have closing dates based on the UTC schedule. The UTC should define closing dates of record two meetings in advance to allow for PRIs to be created late in the cycle. The UTC retains the authority to close these PRIs, as the outcome is the advancement of a proposed draft to a draft, the authorization for final publication, or the abandonment of a proposal, all of which should be reviewed by the UTC.

[183-A148] Action Item for Roozbeh Pournader, Josh Hadley, PAG: Add confusables information based on the disunification of 16 T-source ideographs, based on document IRG N2778 (TCA) and Section 26 of document L2/25-090, for Unicode Version 17.0.
[183-N4] Note: Most disunifications of CJK Unified Ideographs or CJK Compatibility Ideographs result in a new code point being assigned in the Unicode Standard, typically at the end of a CJK Unified Ideographs extension block that may include up to 15 unassigned code points. However, if an existing code point in a CJK Unified Ideographs block can accommodate the disunified ideograph, a new code point is not assigned.
[183-C58] Consensus: Accept the disunification of the KP-source of U+6138, along with the encoding of the disunified ideograph at code point U+2B73F at the end of the CJK Unified Ideographs Extension C block, based on document IRG N2782 (Sim) and as amended in Section 27 of document L2/25-090, for Unicode Version 17.0.

[183-N5] Note: U+2DED4 is not actually a horizontal extension, but rather a source reference change. Its kIRG_GSource property value, GPGLG-3046, was added in Unicode Version 10.0 as part of the CJK Unified Ideographs Extension F block. Its new kIRG_GSource property value per document IRG N2729R3 is GDM-00769. China confirmed on 2025-04-22 that this source reference change is intentional.

[184-C1] Consensus: Approve minutes of UTC meeting #183, correcting note 1 to mark UTR #56 as under the responsibility of the Script Encoding Working Group.
[184-A1] Action Item for Robin Leroy, UTC: Post approved minutes of UTC #183.
[184-N1] Note: The UTC notes with great sadness the tragic loss of Andrew West, who passed away peacefully on July 10, 2025. UTC acknowledges Andrew’s many valuable contributions over 20+ years to the encoding of East Asian scripts. The UTC extends its deepest condolences to Andrew’s family, friends, and his many colleagues around the world.

[184-N2] Note: PAG recommends a conservative approach for Line_Break values of existing characters, including symbols, especially ones that have been encoded for several years. Line_Break value changes should mostly be motivated by reports of actual problems in behavior of specific characters, rather than consistency arguments about broad sets of characters. See L2/25-183 item 4.1.

[187-A147] Action Item for Liang Hai, RMG: Investigate how to manage search engines to ensure reliable indexing of “latest” (unversioned) and versioned components of releases.
[187-C54] Consensus: Starting with Unicode 18.0, publish the code charts traditionally available at unicode.org/charts/(fr/)PDF/U⟨block start⟩.pdf (known internally as the “unversioned” charts) to unicode.org/Public/⟨version number⟩/charts/(fr/)U⟨block start⟩.pdf; the latest charts should remain available at the old URLs as well. See 1.1 in L2/26-101.
[187-C55] Consensus: Starting with Unicode 18.0, updates to the code charts for the current version, once published and available from https://unicode.org/charts/, will not be allowed, except for emergency fixes based on technological defects or serious clerical issues. All prior versions are archived and immutable.
[187-A148] Action Item for Liang Hai, EDC: Revise the core spec and additional documentation about the code charts to reflect the new practice for publishing the charts, for Unicode Version 18.0. This includes new terminology and naming conventions. See 1.1 in L2/26-101.
[187-A149] Action Item for Liang Hai, CHARTS: Right after the release of Unicode 18.0, retroactively archive the 17.0 “unversioned” code charts to unicode.org/Public/17.0.0/charts/U⟨block start⟩.pdf, following the new practice.
[187-A150] Action Item for Liang Hai, CHARTS: After the release of Unicode 18.0, retroactively archive pre-17.0 “unversioned” code charts to unicode.org/Public/⟨version number⟩/charts/U⟨block start⟩.pdf, subject to availability.
[187-C56] Consensus: UTC transfers the ownership of the “auxiliary charts” from the Properties and Algorithms Group to the Charts Working Group. See 1.2 in L2/26-101.
